note: Per pre-registered criteria, candidate C is proposed as the new production candidate, but final adoption is left to the coordinator/user.

v4RerunRationale.reason: T163/T164/T165 landed 3 commits touching engine/ since T158d stage 2 (D4 canonical scheme PWV5, canonical training wiring + PWV6, T165 prerequisite fixes). Rebuilt eval_cli at current HEAD and measured SHA-256 = 9c28701a21e01f371282bf61afd4ed2ccea06d2150555ca3d7cf482bdbc2f970, which differs from T158d's c19f8633ce4f4346ca64a2b5a7c294d4d78e43a9be476de8e923e1056ec3570e. Per T165 review follow-up (c), this precludes reusing T158d's v4 results (wall-clock insurance/timing behavior differences are not guaranteed identical across binary changes), so v4 baseline was re-executed fresh.

crossCheckAgainstT158d.method: compared all 60 games (margin + plies) between this task's fresh v4 run and T158d stage 2's v4 run
crossCheckAgainstT158d.result: 0 mismatches out of 60 -- game outcomes are bit-identical despite the different eval_cli binary SHA, indicating the canonical-scheme additions do not affect legacy PWV3 behavior in practice. This supports (without contradicting) the decision to re-run rather than reuse.

engineDirCommits: 93f085e (T164: canonical training wiring + B3 support, PWV6)

candidateCScalarFeaturePrecheck.method: standalone eval_cli invocation before the 60-game run: `eval_cli gen` to produce the standard initial position, then `eval_cli best --pattern-weights <candidate C path> ...` with the same protocol parameters, capturing stderr
candidateCScalarFeaturePrecheck.note: vs_edax.py's run() helper captures subprocess stderr internally and only surfaces it on failure, so this diagnostic line does not appear in the 60-game run's own logs; the standalone pre-check is the mechanism used to satisfy this requirement.

firstAttemptFailed.reason: worktree was transiently dirty from an in-progress tasks/T166-canonical-game-gate.md work-log edit (documentation only; no effect on engine/vs_edax.py/weights); ensure_clean_worktree() correctly refused to start. Retried with --allow-dirty after confirming the only diff was the tasks/ file.

statistics.boostrapArrayOrderingNote: Opening-level arrays are ordered by opening number ascending (primary-01..primary-30, sorted via `sorted(openings, key=lambda s: int(s.split('-')[1]))`). Game-level arrays are ordered by (opening number ascending, then black before white), via `sorted(keys, key=lambda k: (int(k[0].split('-')[1]), not k[1]))`. This applies to every diffs/game_diffs array fed into paired_bootstrap below (per T162 verifier follow-up request).
statistics.algorithmNote: Identical to bench/edax-compare/compare_pattern_v3.py#paired_bootstrap and to T158d/T162 stage-2 reports: random.Random(seed), resample-with-replacement len(diffs) draws per sample via rng.choice, mean each sample, sort all samples, percentile 2.5/97.5.

referenceComparisons.note: Candidate-vs-candidate paired differences, computed from the same common-opponent (Edax) data via the shared opening set; not part of the pre-registered primary metric but recorded in full per task requirement.

openingLevelRows.description: per-opening (opening, A, B, C, v4) margin averages; A/B/C/v4 keys give the opening-average margin, all 30 openings

selectionRule.criterion: priority = largest point-estimate mean paired difference vs v4 baseline (pre-registered criterion 2)
selectionRule.caveat: B vs C difference (-0.3167, CI [-2.6333, 2.1]) is not statistically significant (sign test p=1.0); the C > B ranking is a formal tie-break on point estimate only, not a demonstrated real advantage.

adoptionCriterionApplication.criterion3aText: selected candidate does not show significant harm vs v4 (CI does not show mean worse than -2 discs AND the entire CI below 0)

candidateC.reason: entire CI is far above 0; this is a strong improvement, not harm

adoptionCriterionApplication.conclusion: Both (a) and (b) are satisfied for candidate C -> proposed as new production candidate per pre-registered rule. Final adoption decision remains with the coordinator/user; candidate B is statistically indistinguishable from C and is an equally supportable alternative.

exactFallbackDefinition.note: Counts ONLY the single transition move (the last budgeted-phase move before the engine's root empties drops to <= unlimited_exact_empties=20). Does not count exactFallback on any other budgeted-phase move earlier in the game. Identical definition to T158d/T162.

excludedGames.reason: engine (candidate A, black) was completely eliminated (0 discs) before the game reached the unlimited-exact phase; legitimate early game termination, not an anomaly (same pattern as T162's v3/primary-30/black)

budgetedToExactDeviation.interpretation: Candidate A shows a notably larger and more variable budgeted-to-exact deviation than the other three (mean 9.31 vs 4.79-5.86), suggesting somewhat less stable midgame evaluation en route to the endgame transition. Candidate C has the smallest deviation of all four (mean 4.79), the most stable.

signFlips.interpretation: All flips originate from small near-zero budgeted values (max magnitude 9.49, candidate B primary-16/white); no catastrophic reversal on any side. B and C show more flips than v4/A, plausibly because they play far closer/more competitive games overall (higher win rates), which mechanically increases the frequency of sign flips near the budgeted/exact boundary -- not evidence of search instability.

watchPoint.candidateCScalarFeatureEffectCheck: Confirmed active pre-flight (see candidateCScalarFeaturePrecheck above). Candidate C's strong result (mean -6.75 vs Edax, +17.37 vs v4) demonstrates the B3-canonical evaluation (including its scalar features) functions correctly in practice, though C is not statistically distinguishable from B (pure pattern canonical), so this run alone does not isolate how much the scalar features specifically contribute beyond the canonical pattern improvement itself.

anomalyCheck.earlyTerminationNote: candidate A game primary-30/black ended early due to legitimate complete elimination (0 discs, no legal moves), not an anomaly

proposal: Candidate C (Egaroucid B3-canonical seed1) is proposed as the new production candidate per pre-registered criteria (largest point estimate vs v4, CI entirely above 0, 0 anomalies). Candidate B (Egaroucid v4-canonical seed3) is statistically indistinguishable from C and is an equally valid alternative. Candidate A (WTHOR v4-canonical seed2) shows no significant difference from v4 and is not competitive with B/C.
outOfScope: production wiring (WASM PWV5/PWV6 support, ANALYSIS_ENGINE_VERSION bump, Pages verification -- deferred to T167)
